title: GAIA project-config.yaml — full structural schema v2.0.0 (E85-S2)
description: JSON Schema (draft-07) for the canonical project-config.yaml surface. v2.0.0 introduces the `config_phase` state machine (minimal -> partial -> full) and `schema_version` field per ADR-096/ADR-097. Conditional section requirements via `allOf`+`if/then` blocks: `minimal` requires only project_name + project_kind; `partial` adds stacks + platforms; `full` adds environments + ci_cd. The absence of `config_phase` (legacy configs) is treated as `full` by gate-side code per NFR-062 (backward compatibility) — the schema enforces this by NOT placing `config_phase` in `required` and by guarding every `if` with `required:[config_phase]` so absence skips all conditional blocks. Covers the eleven new top-level sections from E68-S1 (FR-RSV2-5..22) plus the existing surface. Designed for offline validation via `npx ajv-cli validate -s project-config.schema.json -d project-config.json`. NFR-RSV2-4 / ADR-079 require schema-validation discipline; this file extends the same load-time-validation pattern used by analysis-results.schema.json. Credential values literally embedded in environments.*.credentials.* are rejected via a pattern-based deny-list per AC11 / FR-RSV2-9.

credentialEnvVarRef.description: An environment variable reference holding a credential. MUST be the NAME of an env var (e.g., DB_PASSWORD_VAR) and MUST NOT be a literal credential. Common-secret patterns (sk-, ghp_, AKIA, xox, glpat-) and credential-style key=value strings are rejected so a project-config.yaml accidentally committed with a literal secret fails validation per FR-RSV2-9 / AC11.

platformId.description: Platform identifier. Documented values are `web`, `ios`, `android`, `server` (E68-S1 baseline + AF-2026-05-30-2 / Test10 F-05 headless). `server` represents headless backend services with no UI surface — its presence in a full-mode config satisfies the platforms minItems:1 constraint for backend-only projects (the shape the brownfield tutorial tells users to declare). The schema enforces this strict enum so typos and out-of-domain identifiers (e.g., `windows`, `linux`) are rejected at validation time. Per ADR-081 §4.2 the surface is EXTENSIBLE at the resolver layer — `resolve-config.sh` emits a stderr warning (rather than aborting) for unknown identifiers that match the kebab-case pattern, so a project on a stale schema-version that adds a future identifier (e.g., `harmonyos`) is not blocked. Schema changes are still REQUIRED to formally document a new platform, but downstream consumers degrade gracefully in the meantime.

formFactor.description: Device form-factor enum. Intentionally broader than the current iOS / Android scope to support future wearable / TV adapters without schema-version bumps.

screenSize.description: Logical screen-size descriptor. `width` and `height` are integer points; `density` is a float pixel-ratio (e.g., 2.0 Retina, 3.0 iPhone Plus, 2.625 typical Android xxhdpi).

deviceTargetEntry.description: Per-platform device matrix entry. `os_versions` are free-form version strings (semver, marketing version, or major-only — no canonical form imposed). `screen_sizes` is the device-grid the mobile rubrics iterate over. `form_factors` is the enumerated list of device classes the project ships against.

healthCheck.description: Post-deploy health-check phase configuration (FR-425, E78-S3). Drives the `/gaia-deploy` Phase 3 behavior. Optional — backward compatibility is preserved when the key is omitted (resolver defaults to mode=poll, which matches pre-FR-425 behavior). The `skip` mode is intended for projects that have no reachable health-check endpoint (e.g., marketplace-published plugins) — when set, the deploy skill bypasses the poll loop and writes an audit-trail evidence entry recording the configured skip.

mode.description: Health-check execution mode. `poll` (default) runs the existing exponential-backoff poll loop against `environments.<env>.url`. `skip` bypasses the poll loop entirely and records `{mode: skip, reason: "configured skip"}` to the evidence directory. Any value other than these two MUST be rejected at config-load time.

distributionChannel.description: A single distribution channel entry (FR-424, E78-S2). Declarative configuration for marketplace publishing — `type` is intentionally generic (e.g., `marketplace` rather than `github` or `npm`) so future channel types (`registry`, `cdn`, etc.) can be added to the enum without breaking existing configs. `version_file` is a flat filesystem path (NOT a JSON Pointer); the resolved deploy_adapter is responsible for reading the file and extracting `version_key` from it. `additionalProperties: false` prevents typo'd field names from silently passing validation.

type.description: Generic channel type. Intentionally NOT vendor-specific — the value is `marketplace`, not `github` or `npm`. Future types may be added to the enum without breaking existing configs.

provider.description: Concrete release backend identifier (e.g., `github-releases`). Free-form string; the resolved deploy_adapter interprets it.

deploy_adapter.description: Adapter name resolved by /gaia-deploy under plugins/gaia/scripts/adapters/<deploy_adapter>/. Documented value: `marketplace-publish` (E78-S1).

repository.description: Target repository in `owner/repo` format. Pattern matches GitHub repository naming rules (alphanumeric, dot, hyphen, underscore, exactly one slash).

version_file.description: Flat filesystem path to the file holding the authoritative version (e.g., `package.json`, `manifest.yaml`). MUST NOT be a JSON Pointer — the leading `#` form (e.g., `#/version`) is rejected so the schema stays decoupled from any particular file format. The resolved deploy_adapter reads the file and extracts `version_key` from it.

version_key.description: Key within `version_file` that holds the version value (e.g., `version`).

release_notes_source.description: Relative file path to release notes. Conventional default: `CHANGELOG.md`.

smoke_test.description: Post-deploy smoke verification mode for this channel. `manual-checklist` signals that smoke is a manual approval step rather than automated — coordinates with E78-S5 (empty `smoke_suites` handling).

lifecycle.description: ADR-120 lifecycle-ordering enforcement settings. Currently exposes `strict_mode` only; future fields land in this object so the top-level surface stays minimal. `strict_mode: true` (default per ADR-120) causes E103-S3/S4/S6 gates to hard-error on missing MANDATORY upstream artifacts unless a `--bypass <skill> --reason "<text>"` record exists in `.gaia/state/lifecycle-overrides.yaml`. `strict_mode: false` downgrades the gates to advisory warnings. Opted out of config-hydration auto-allowlist (operator-managed only).

strict_mode.description: When true (default), E103 gates hard-error on missing MANDATORY upstream artifacts. When false, gates downgrade to WARNING logs.

config_phase.description: Config-phase state machine value per ADR-096. Enum: minimal | partial | full. `default: "full"` is a documentation hint — gate-side code treats absence as `full` per ADR-097 (absence-over-sentinel) and NFR-062 (backward compatibility). The conditional `required` blocks in `allOf` are guarded by `required:[config_phase]` so legacy configs without this field skip all phase-conditional requirements.

schema_version.description: Schema version string per ADR-096 D4 (semver format, e.g., "2.0.0"). Optional for backward compatibility but SHOULD be present in all new configs. Top-level `string` property — distinct from JSON Schema's `$schema` / `$id` metadata fields.

project_name.description: Human-readable project name (referenced by conditional `required` blocks). Optional for legacy configs; required at all config_phase values per the conditional blocks.

template_overrides.description: Declarative overrides applied to generated workflows during /gaia-config-ci --regenerate (FR-518, ADR-114 §(e), E98-S4). Three fields: disable (array of job names), timeout_overrides (map of job-name → integer minutes), adapter_versions (map of adapter-name → semver). The disable: array is gated by the SR-78 closed-enum of security-critical job names — see plugins/gaia/scripts/lib/template-overrides.sh for the canonical list.

items.description: Job name to remove. The five SR-78 security-critical names (commitlint, adr-048-guard, no-claude-attribution, secrets-scan, nfr-082-credential-audit) are REJECTED at /gaia-config-validate time per threat T-TOV-1. Note: the schema enforces the LITERAL form only — hyphen+case canonicalization (e.g. `commit-lint` → `commitlint`) happens at regen time in template-overrides.sh as a defense-in-depth layer.

disable.description: Job names to remove from the generated workflow's jobs: map. Names in the SR-78 closed enum (commitlint, adr-048-guard, no-claude-attribution, secrets-scan, nfr-082-credential-audit) are REJECTED both at /gaia-config-validate time (schema-level not.enum) and at regen time (template-overrides.sh, with hyphen+case canonicalization for defense in depth) per threat T-TOV-1.

timeout_overrides.description: Per-job timeout-minutes override. Values outside [1, 360] are HARD ERROR at regen time.

adapter_versions.description: Adapter-name → semver pin (e.g. {markdownlint: "0.41.0"}). Unknown adapter names and unparseable semver values are HARD ERROR at regen time.

compliance.description: Opt-in regulatory regime selection (FR-RSV2-5, FR-RSV2-7). The compliance section itself is optional at the top-level; an absent section is treated semantically as `regimes: [], ui_present: false` (E71-S9 AC4). An explicit empty `regimes: []` is also accepted.

brownfield.description: AF-2026-05-30-2 / Test10 F-06 — brownfield deterministic-tools configuration. Edited via /gaia-config-brownfield. The YAML descriptor schema at plugins/gaia/config/project-config.schema.yaml declares the same section with the full nested-key catalog (deterministic_tools, prewarm_enabled, sarif_merge_enabled, dedup_enabled, grype_enabled, sbom_completeness_enabled, detect_signals_enabled, deadcode_go_enabled, deadcode_python_enabled, deadcode_jvm_enabled, phase_4b_cross_stack_enabled, phase_4b_enabled, defectdojo_enabled, tools.runner, scanner_tier). This JSON-schema entry permits the section's presence; the YAML schema is the source of truth for inner key validation.

test_execution.description: Pipeline-tier placement + runner command for the three test tiers (FR-RSV2-11, AF-2026-05-22-6). Each tier MAY carry: `placement` (which pipeline stage runs the tier), `command` (the runner command-string the bridge dispatcher executes), and `timeout_seconds` (per-tier execution budget). The bridge dispatcher (`scripts/run-tests.sh`) reads `command` and `timeout_seconds` — without these the bridge emits a `no command declared` skip-evidence and the run never drives the test runner. Pre-AF-22-6 the schema disallowed `command`/`timeout_seconds`, so the dispatcher could only ever skip.

sprint_review.description: Per-stack execution-review configuration consumed by /gaia-sprint-review Track B (E93-S2 / E93-S4). The playwright_headed key is constrained to `true` at schema level (NFR-069 / T-SGR-2 — foreground-mode enforcement is unbypassable). Unknown stack identifiers in the *_commands maps emit a WARNING from /gaia-config-validate but do NOT fail schema validation (ADR-081 §4.2 forward-compatibility precedent). Section is classified in _CONFIG_HYDRATION_MANAGED_ELSEWHERE (config-hydration.sh) — human-managed via /gaia-config-sprint-review only; never written by the auto-hydration reconciler.

backend_commands.description: Map: backend stack identifier (e.g., backend-python, backend-node, backend-go) → command-string. Unknown identifiers WARN.

frontend_command.description: DEPRECATED (issue #1047, AF-2026-06-01-4) — single command-string for the frontend Playwright execution-review run. Kept as a backward-compatible alias for single-web-stack projects. Prefer `frontend_commands` (map) for projects with more than one web/front-end stack. When both are set, `frontend_commands` entries take precedence; the scalar is merged in as the synthetic stack-id `frontend` only when that key is absent from the map.

frontend_commands.description: Map: frontend / web stack identifier (e.g., frontend, website, marketing-site) → command-string. Mirrors backend_commands / mobile_commands / desktop_commands / plugin_commands. Unknown identifiers WARN (ADR-081 §4.2). Added by AF-2026-06-01-4 (issue #1047) to lift the single-web-stack cap baked into the legacy `frontend_command` scalar.

mobile_commands.description: Map: mobile stack identifier (e.g., mobile-react-native, mobile-flutter) → command-string. Unknown identifiers WARN.

desktop_commands.description: Map: desktop stack identifier (electron | tauri | native) → command-string. Unknown identifiers WARN.

plugin_commands.description: Map: plugin stack identifier (claude-code | vscode) → command-string. Unknown identifiers WARN.

playwright_headed.description: Foreground-mode enforcement (NFR-069 / T-SGR-2). MUST be true. The schema-level `const: true` ensures /gaia-config-validate REJECTS — not just warns — on `playwright_headed: false`, closing the silent-bypass attack vector.

timeout_per_stack.description: Per-stack wall-clock timeout (seconds). Default 300 (5 min). Minimum 30, maximum 3600.

human_confirm.description: Whether human confirmation is required after each stack's execution-review run. `required` halts for user input; `optional` auto-continues with a logged WARNING (only set this in CI bridge fixtures).

screen_recording_fallback.description: Whether the Track B runner falls back to screen-recording capture when Playwright cannot drive the stack (e.g., native-desktop). Default true.

gates.description: Per-gate severity overrides (FR-RSV2-12). Each gate may override the global severity mapping.

stacks.description: Ordered list of stack-path rules for multi-service repos (FR-RSV2-6). The four additive properties `path`, `excludes`, `cross_refs`, and `ignore_nested_manifests` (FR-546 / ADR-126) extend each entry for multi-stack monorepo path partitioning; all four are OPTIONAL with safe defaults so pre-deploy entries validate byte-compatible (zero-regression invariant for the single-stack majority).

path.description: Stack ROOT directory — a coarse-grained partitioning anchor (FR-546 / ADR-126). Distinct from `paths` (the in-stack glob list): the deterministic-tools orchestrator scopes adapter dispatch to this subtree BEFORE applying `paths` glob matching. Default null (unset) — single-stack repos omit it entirely.

excludes.description: Glob patterns excluded from this stack's file set (ADR-126). Defaults to the SR-87 secret-file patterns (`.env`, `.env.*`, `secrets/`, `*.pem`, `*.key`) when seeded by /gaia-init, reducing the chance of credentials leaking into deterministic-tool scans.

cross_refs.description: Allowed cross-stack references — each entry MUST name another stack in this `stacks[]` list (ADR-126 / E104-S5 Phase 4b cross-stack WARNING allowlist). Referential integrity (entry names an existing stack) is enforced as a post-validation pass in /gaia-config-stack and /gaia-config-validate, NOT in this schema (JSON Schema cannot express cross-property validation).

ignore_nested_manifests.description: When true (default), ecosystem-manifest auto-detection ignores manifests nested under this stack's `path` so a monorepo subtree is not double-counted as its own stack (ADR-126 / E70-S11).

cross_service_tests.description: Contract and cross-service integration test configuration.

environments.description: Environment list with env-var-only credential references (FR-RSV2-9, NFR-RSV2-7) and a 3-value kind discriminator per FR-520 / ADR-112 §(a). Two shapes accepted: (a) the legacy map-of-entries (`environments: {staging: {...}, prod: {...}}`) preserved for back-compat; (b) the FR-520 array-of-entries with `id` + closed-enum `kind` per E99-S1.

kind.description: FR-520 / ADR-112 §(a) closed 3-value discriminator. Field is optional — read-time default is `deployable` per NFR-080 silent back-compat (resolved by scripts/lib/resolve-env-kind.sh). Unknown values are rejected at schema-validation time (E99-S1 AC6 / TC-EKD-6).

ci_platform.description: CI provider selection and pipeline pointer.

platforms.description: List of platform identifiers. Presence of ios or android triggers mobile gates downstream (FR-RSV2-26).

project_kind.description: Project shape identifier (FR-403, ADR-087). Foundational axis for the review / test / CI / deploy pipeline to branch behavior by project type. Documented values: claude-code-plugin, web-app, mobile-app, api, library — but the schema deliberately omits an enum constraint so the field is open-vocabulary. resolve-config.sh emits a stderr WARNING for unrecognized values (advisory, non-blocking) per ADR-087's unknown-value advisory pattern. Optional — backward compatibility is preserved when the field is omitted (no warning, no functional regression).

device_targets.description: Device/OS matrix for mobile platforms (FR-RSV2-27). Keyed by platform identifier — keys SHOULD match entries in `platforms[]`; orphan keys (e.g., `device_targets.ios` when `platforms: [android]`) are flagged at the resolver layer, not the schema (open `additionalProperties` mirrors the extensibility principle on `platforms`). Each value is a `deviceTargetEntry` requiring `os_versions`, `screen_sizes`, and `form_factors` per ADR-081.

distribution.description: Distribution / publish configuration. Two shapes accepted via oneOf: (a) legacy `channels[]` array per E78-S2 / FR-424; (b) FR-521 / ADR-112 §(b) single-channel shape with 4 required common fields (channel, registry, manifest, release_workflow) plus per-channel sub-fields. The new (b) shape is the canonical surface for /gaia-publish (E100); the (a) shape is preserved for back-compat. Optional — backward compatibility is preserved when the key is omitted (no functional regression).

channel.description: FR-522 / ADR-112 §(c) closed 10-channel enum. Adding a named channel is a future ADR amendment + schema bump; do NOT extend this list inline.

registry.description: Free-form registry URL or identifier (e.g., https://registry.npmjs.org, ghcr.io/myorg/myimage).

manifest.description: Relative path to the version-bearing manifest file (e.g., plugin.json, package.json, Cargo.toml).

release_workflow.description: Relative path or name of the workflow under .github/workflows/ that publishes the artifact.

adapter_name.description: custom required sub-field — names the directory under .gaia/custom/adapters/publish-<adapter_name>/. SR-81: regex bars slashes / dots / uppercase to prevent path-traversal (T-DCH-3).

allOf.description: config_phase=minimal — Phase 0 bootstrap. Only project_name + project_kind are required (in addition to the unconditional infrastructure fields).
config_phase=partial — architecture-phase hydration complete. Adds stacks + platforms to the required set.

config_phase=full — infrastructure-phase hydration complete. Adds environments + ci_cd to the required set. Legacy configs without config_phase are NOT routed through this branch (the `required:[config_phase]` guard ensures absence skips); gate-side code treats absence-means-full per NFR-062.
